title: Custom Greeting Context Manager
description: Write a class called Greeting that acts as a context manager. Its __enter__ method should print 'Hello' and return self. Its __exit__ method should print 'Goodbye'. Then use the with statement to create a Greeting instance and print 'Inside' inside the block. The output must be exactly Hello, Inside, Goodbye — one per line.
hints: In __enter__, use print('Hello') and then return self
In __exit__, use print('Goodbye') — you can ignore the parameters for now
The with Greeting(): block is already written for you — just fill in the print statements

code_requirements.description: Uses the with keyword to invoke a context manager
code_requirements.fail_message: You must use the 'with' keyword to invoke your Greeting context manager.
